headline: Graduate Overqualification Rate Hits 38% in Service Sector
context: DOSM labor force data shows 38% of degree holders work in jobs requiring only SPM-level qualifications.

cards.big: 38% of Malaysian degree holders work in jobs that only require SPM qualifications
cards.sub: Overqualification wastes years of education investment for graduates and taxpayers

cards.big: Average salary for overqualified graduates is RM2,200 versus RM3,400 for matched graduates
cards.sub: RM1,200 monthly penalty for degree-job mismatch compounds over entire careers

cards.big: Malaysia produces 250,000 graduates annually while economy creates only 155,000 graduate-level jobs
cards.sub: Structural oversupply of degrees exceeds labor market absorption capacity

cards.big: Germany limits university enrollment in oversupplied fields through numerus clausus system
cards.sub: Supply management prevents systematic overproduction of graduates in low-demand fields

cards.big: A degree that leads to a job below qualification is an investment with negative returns
Should Malaysia restrict enrollment in programs with persistent low employment outcomes
